data.title: Machi Brother's 13-game winning streak was broken, and he closed his BTC long positions, incurring a loss of $192,000.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, Machi Big Brother's (@machibigbrother) 13-game winning streak has been broken. Due to the market decline, he just closed his BTC long positions, incurring a loss of $192,000.

data.title: US-Iran negotiations: Though neither broken down nor broken through, diplomatic efforts continue.
data.description: According to Al Jazeera, despite numerous efforts from all sides, the US and Iran have yet to reach an agreement. A compromise exists on the table. Vice President Vance's statement suggests that some possibility remains—he indicated it was their final and best solution. A source stated that the good news is that negotiations have not broken down, but the bad news is that no breakthrough has been achieved. It is understood that approximately five rounds of talks have been held, with written exchanges of views following each round, suggesting that a broader framework is in place. Currently, there is one proposal and one counter-proposal. We need to pay attention to Iran's response, as the interviewed source believes the US has made maximum demands, particularly regarding the Strait of Hormuz, on which Iran is unwilling to concede.

data.title: U.S. Delegation Leaves Pakistan Without Agreement in Iran Talks
data.description: On April 12, a U.S. delegation departed Pakistan after negotiations with Iran failed to reach an agreement. According to BlockBeats, the discussions did not yield any conclusive results. The talks were part of ongoing efforts to address issues between the two nations. The departure marks another chapter in the complex diplomatic relations involving the United States and Iran.

data.title: Crypto Whale Adjusts Stop-Loss to Secure Profits
data.description:
On April 12, a prominent crypto trader known as 'Set 10 Big Goals' (@Jason60704294) announced adjustments to their stop-loss positions to prevent targeted liquidation. According to BlockBeats, the trader has already secured profits from their current short positions.

Previously, it was reported that the trader held a short position of 2,567.49 BTC with an entry price of $71,554.61, and a short position of 38,465.22 ETH with an entry price of $2,248.74.

data.title: Bitcoin Drops 2% Amid U.S.-Iran Negotiation Stalemate
data.description:
Bitcoin experienced a decline of approximately 2%, settling around $71,600 following a statement by Vice President J.D. Vance. According to NS3.AI, the announcement revealed that U.S. and Iranian negotiators were unable to reach an agreement on extending a ceasefire. This development occurred after a nearly six-week U.S. campaign against Iran, with talks taking place in Pakistan.

In the wake of Vance's press conference, Ethereum (ETH) also saw a decrease, falling to about $2,200. XRP dropped to $1.33, and the CoinDesk 20 index fell to 1,188.52. The market's reaction highlights the sensitivity of cryptocurrency prices to geopolitical events and negotiations.

data.title: Analyst Discusses Stop-Loss Adjustment Strategy
data.description: An analyst, @ai_9684xtpa, posted on X about the rationale behind adjusting a stop-loss point to $73,500. The adjustment was made to prevent precise liquidation, and the analyst noted that the price nearly reached this adjusted level.

data.title: Iran-U.S. Negotiations End Without Agreement Due to Disputes
data.description: Negotiations between Iran and the United States concluded without reaching an agreement, according to reports from Iran on April 12. According to BlockBeats, the discussions ended in the early hours of April 12 local time, with no consensus achieved due to what was described as 'U.S. greed and ambition.'

data.title: Iran says negotiations have ended without an agreement with the United States.
data.description: According to Iran's Tasnim News Agency, citing a reporter, negotiations between Iran and the United States have ended without an agreement. The US's excessive demands hindered the formation of a common framework and agreement. (Jinshi)

data.title: X Platform Enhances Revenue Sharing for Original Content Creators
data.description: Nikita Bier, head of product at X, announced efforts to identify original content creators for revenue sharing in the current cycle. According to PANews, Bier emphasized that reposts and comments remain central to the X platform, but the revenue sharing initiative aims to motivate creators to produce high-quality original content, adding new value to the Timeline. The platform plans to continually improve the revenue sharing scheme and encourage creators to publish top-quality content on X.

data.title: US Vice President Vance: No agreement has been reached; Iran has chosen not to accept the US conditions.
data.description: US Vice President Vance: Twenty-one hours of negotiations with Iran have not yet resulted in an agreement; Iran has chosen not to accept the US conditions. (Jinshi)

data.title: BNB Drops Below 600 USDT with a 0.73% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 12, 2026, 01:37 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has dropped below 600 USDT and is now trading at 599.51001 USDT, with a narrowed 0.73% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 72,000 USDT with a 1.23% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 12, 2026, 01:42 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 72,000 USDT and is now trading at 71,964.390625 USDT, with a narrowed 1.23% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: A whale controlling 59% of the circulating supply of NOM transferred its last 278 million NOM tokens to Binance.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, an hour ago, a whale or institution controlling 59% of the circulating supply of NOM transferred its last 278 million NOM tokens, worth $1.24 million, to Binance. This brings the total number of NOM tokens withdrawn from Binance on April 1st through seven wallets to Binance. During the period of the NOM transfer to Binance, its price dropped from $0.007 to $0.004, a decrease of 45%.

data.title: Despite a loss of nearly $5 billion in 2025, Musk's SpaceX will still hold $603 million worth of Bitcoin.
data.description: According to data from Arkham and a report published Friday evening by The Information, SpaceX currently holds 8,285 bitcoins in its Coinbase Prime escrow account, worth approximately $603 million, while the company is projected to lose nearly $5 billion in 2025. This loss is significantly different from the previous year, when SpaceX achieved a profit of approximately $8 billion, with revenue estimated between $15 billion and $16 billion. While revenue is projected to increase to $18.5 billion in 2025, the integration costs from the merger of Musk's AI company xAI into SpaceX in February of this year outweigh the revenue growth. Despite the substantial losses, SpaceX's bitcoin holdings have remained unchanged. CoinDesk's analysis of transaction records shows that the most recent significant fund movement occurred approximately four months ago, a rebalancing operation involving the transfer of 614 bitcoins and 1,021 bitcoins between SpaceX's internal wallets. The holdings have remained stable since mid-2024, and previously exceeded $1.6 billion in market value during Bitcoin's all-time high in October 2025. SpaceX is currently the fourth largest known company in terms of Bitcoin holdings, after Strategy, Marathon Digital, and Riot Platforms. SpaceX has filed for an IPO, at which time its Bitcoin holdings will appear in public filings for the first time and may be required to be valued at fair value under the new FASB guidelines that will take effect at the end of 2025.

data.title: The whale nemorino.eth, which established a position 5 days ago, sold 2,950 ETH, making a profit of $267,000.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, nemorino.eth (0x8Ae...0E0D) sold 2,950 ETH at $2,304.40 four hours ago, worth $6.798 million. This portion of ETH was bought at $2,2130 between April 7th and April 8th. The sale has resulted in a profit of $267,000 on the sold portion, with the remaining portion showing a floating profit of $72,000.

data.title: Iran reports on progress of negotiations
data.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Baghae briefed the media early this morning (April 12) on the progress of negotiations with the United States in Islamabad, Pakistan. According to his briefing, negotiations between Iran and the United States, mediated by Pakistan, began on the morning of April 11 and are still ongoing. The two sides have exchanged a great deal of information, and Iranian negotiators are “doing their utmost to protect Iran’s interests.” The briefing stated that the two sides discussed various aspects of the main negotiating issues, including the Strait of Hormuz, the nuclear issue, war reparations, the lifting of sanctions, and a complete end to the war against Iran and the Middle East. The briefing emphasized that the success of this diplomatic process depends on the sincerity of the United States, whether it makes excessive and illegal demands, and whether it accepts Iran's legitimate rights. (CCTV International News)

data.title: Ether Machine Cancels $1.6 Billion SPAC Merger with Dynamix Due to Market Conditions
data.description: The Ether Machine has decided to terminate its planned $1.6 billion SPAC merger with Dynamix, citing unfavorable market conditions. According to NS3.AI, the merger was intended to list the ETH treasury firm on Nasdaq under the ticker ETHM. As part of the termination agreement, an SEC filing indicates that Dynamix will receive a $50 million payment within 15 days.

data.title: Aave V4 Increases Deposit Limits to Accommodate Additional Liquidity
data.description: Aave has announced an increase in deposit limits for its V4 platform due to several assets reaching maximum capacity. According to Foresight News, the platform aims to accommodate additional liquidity by raising these limits. Furthermore, Aave plans to gradually adjust deposit and borrowing limits as funds continue to fill.

data.title: EU Proposes Regulatory Shift for Cross-Border Institutions
data.description:
The European Union is considering a significant change in its regulatory framework for cross-border institutions. According to ChainCatcher, the proposal suggests transferring regulatory authority from national supervisory bodies to the European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA). This shift would affect large Crypto Asset Service Providers (CASPs), trading venues, central counterparties, and central securities depositories.

This proposal marks the most substantial structural adjustment in the EU's crypto regulatory framework since the Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) was fully implemented for CASPs at the end of 2024. Under the current MiCA framework, national authorities serve as the primary regulators, while ESMA plays a coordinating role.
